summary: PR #2499 made focused lbarq decomp-matching/type cleanup in src/melee/lb/lbarq.c and src/melee/lb/lbarq.h: it introduced concrete lbArq state names, typed the node state field and accessor return, replaced opaque global padding with a 10-node pool, used enum state indices for the three lists, kept lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line with a narrow dont_inline pragma, and added PAD_STACK in lbArq_80014BD0. The PR body reports two 100% functions and two partial matches, while a bot report confirms an improvement for lbArq_80014BD0 from 93.97% to 96.55%. The PR was closed with merged_at null in the supplied metadata.

key_files.role: Implementation updates for lbarq list/state handling, typed state access, out-of-line accessor pragma, PAD_STACK in request path, and enum state use in initialization and transitions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c hunks for lbArq_80014ABC, lbArq_80014AC4, lbArq_80014BD0, lbArq_80014D2C

key_files.role: Header updates defining lbArqState, typing lbArqNode.state, replacing lbArqGlobal padding with nodes[10], and changing lbArq_80014ABC prototype to return lbArqState.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.h enum lbArqState and lbArqGlobal nodes[10] changes
human_pr_text: Summary says concrete lbArq state/list types and 10-node pool

what_changed.text: Added enum lbArqState with LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE = 0, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING = 1, and LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE = 2, then replaced raw state/list indices with those names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.c replacements of list[0]/list[1]/list[2] and state 0/1/2

what_changed.text: Changed lbArqNode.state from u32 to lbArqState and changed lbArq_80014ABC from returning u32 to returning lbArqState.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbarq.h lbArqNode state field and lbArq_80014ABC prototype

what_changed.text: Replaced lbArqGlobal's opaque pad[0x1E0] with lbArqNode nodes[10], and changed initialization to use global->nodes instead of casting the global object to lbArqNode*.
Kept lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line by wrapping it in #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says keep lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line

what_changed.text: Added PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 and retained PAD_STACK(8) in lbArq_80014D2C.

Reported matching status: lbArq_80014ABC and lbArq_80014D2C at 100%; lbArq_80014AC4 at 96.9403%; lbArq_80014BD0 at 96.55173%. The bot report independently lists lbArq_80014BD0 improving from 93.97% to 96.55%.

smart_moves.text: Recovered a real typed shape for the lbarq global pool instead of preserving opaque padding and casts.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says replace opaque global padding with the 10-node pool
raw_diff: lbArqGlobal pad[0x1E0] replaced by lbArqNode nodes[10]

smart_moves.text: Named the small state domain and used the enum both for node state values and list indexing, making the free/pending/done list logic searchable and reviewable.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: enum lbArqState and LB_ARQ_STATE_* replacements
human_pr_text: Summary says name the verified ARQ node states used by lbarq

smart_moves.text: Kept a narrow out-of-line tactic localized to lbArq_80014ABC rather than broad pragma use.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: pragma push/dont_inline/pop around only lbArq_80014ABC
human_pr_text: Summary says keep lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an opaque global blob is used as an array of records and local initialization walks a fixed-size pool, recovering the concrete pool type can remove casts and improve both source clarity and matching work.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: lbArqGlobal pad[0x1E0] to lbArqNode nodes[10]
raw_diff: nodes = global->nodes instead of (lbArqNode*) global

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small integer state fields that also select list slots are good enum candidates when the PR evidence names the states and all observed uses are the same state domain.
For matching tactics such as PAD_STACK or dont_inline, keep the tactic narrow and attach the exact function-level checkdiff evidence claimed by the PR body.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification commands for lbArq_80014ABC and lbArq_80014BD0

naming_conventions.name: lbArqState
naming_conventions.rationale: Matches the existing lbArq prefix and describes the node/list state domain introduced by the PR.

naming_conventions.name: LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE
naming_conventions.rationale: Names raw state/list index 0 as the free list state.

naming_conventions.name: LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING
naming_conventions.rationale: Names raw state/list index 1 as the pending list state.

naming_conventions.name: LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE
naming_conventions.rationale: Names raw state/list index 2 as the done list state.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: global->list[LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE] and wait loop compares against LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE

naming_conventions.name: nodes
naming_conventions.rationale: Replaces opaque padding in lbArqGlobal with the 10-node pool named in the PR body.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use #pragma dont_inline on around lbArq_80014ABC with push/pop scope to keep the accessor out-of-line.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says keep lbArq_80014ABC out-of-line
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_constraint: Global standards treat pragmas as exceptions; this PR evidence supports only this narrow function scope, not broad pragma use.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0 to improve stack/codegen.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary says improve lbArq_80014BD0 stack/codegen
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report shows lbArq_80014BD0 improvement from 93.97% to 96.55%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_constraint: PR body reports remaining drift; PAD_STACK is evidence-backed here by reported checkdiff/bot output but should not be generalized without local objdiff evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Preserve list-walk loops while changing raw numeric list indices to enum constants.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: while (*tail != NULL) list traversal remains while list indices become LB_ARQ_STATE_*
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk_or_constraint: No evidence in the slice that alternate loop forms were tested; only the enum-index cleanup is supported.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report lists one improvement in unmatched item main/melee/lb/lbarq: lbArq_80014BD0, +10 bytes, before 93.97%, after 96.55%.

follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for lbArqState or LB_ARQ_STATE_* to see whether these names/types landed elsewhere after this closed unmerged PR.
Search lbarq current source for lbArqGlobal nodes[10] versus pad[0x1E0] to verify current accepted source state before promoting any path fact.
Search for PAD_STACK in lbArq_80014BD0 and nearby lb functions to compare whether this tactic survived review or was replaced.
Search for pragma dont_inline around small accessors in lb library code before deriving any broader convention.
Search issue or review history around PR #2499 closure if curator needs to know why it closed unmerged.

notes: The diff and PR body provide strong evidence for changed files, type/name edits, and claimed checkdiff commands.
The supplied metadata says state CLOSED and merged_at null; do not infer merge acceptance.

The PR body claims configure.py && ninja and per-function checkdiff commands, but the slice does not include raw checkdiff JSON output or build logs.
Bot report only confirms the lbArq_80014BD0 percentage improvement; it does not confirm the other function percentages.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover concrete pool structs from opaque global padding when initialization proves a fixed node pool
accepted_candidate_records.text: In lbarq, replacing lbArqGlobal pad[0x1E0] with lbArqNode nodes[10] allowed code to use global->nodes rather than casting the global object to lbArqNode*. This is a useful typed-fields lesson, but should be scoped to evidence-backed fixed pools.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Narrow dont_inline pragma for lbArq_80014ABC accessor
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2499 used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop only around lbArq_80014ABC, and the PR body reported lbArq_80014ABC at 100%. Treat this as a narrow, evidence-bound tactic, not a general style rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK in lbArq_80014BD0 improved reported match percentage
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2499 added PAD_STACK(16) in lbArq_80014BD0. The PR body reported 96.55173% for the function and the bot report showed an improvement from 93.97% to 96.55%. Keep such stack tactics narrow and tied to checkdiff evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Possible lbarq global layout: lbArqGlobal contains lbArqNode nodes[10] followed by three state lists
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2499 proposed lbArqGlobal as lbArqNode nodes[10] at offset 0x000 and lbArqNode* list[3] at offset 0x1E0, replacing u8 pad[0x1E0]. Because the PR is closed with merged_at null in the slice, verify current source/symbol evidence before accepting this as a path fact.
evidence_refs: metadata_json: state CLOSED and merged_at null
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful type/layout fact, but PR closure without merge evidence requires curator verification against current source.

source_update_candidates.title: Possible lbarq state enum names FREE/PENDING/DONE
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2499 proposed lbArqState with LB_ARQ_STATE_FREE, LB_ARQ_STATE_PENDING, and LB_ARQ_STATE_DONE corresponding to values 0, 1, and 2, and used them for node->state and global->list indices. Verify whether this naming landed before adding to source-owned path facts.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: enum constants used in lbarq.c
metadata_json: state CLOSED and merged_at null
source_update_candidates.reason: Good naming candidate supported by PR diff, but not accepted as current source fact from this slice alone.

source_update_candidates.title: Cautionary example for narrow pragma and PAD_STACK tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2499 is a candidate example for the existing standards on narrow matching tactics: dont_inline was scoped with push/pop around one accessor, and PAD_STACK(16) was tied to lbArq_80014BD0 matching evidence. This should be curated as an example only if current maintainers consider the closed PR useful precedent.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification commands and matching percentages
source_update_candidates.reason: Could enrich standards with a concrete historical example, but the slice does not justify changing global standards.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the lbArqGlobal nodes[10] layout or lbArqState names as accepted current path facts from this PR alone because the PR is closed and merged_at is null.
Do not infer why the PR was closed; the slice contains no human review or closure explanation.
Do not generalize PAD_STACK or dont_inline as preferred style; standards require narrow evidence and this slice only supports the specific functions touched.
Do not claim all matching percentages were independently verified by bot output; only lbArq_80014BD0 has a loaded bot report.
